name: Jupyter On Slurm
summary: Launch Jupyter notebooks inside short Slurm compute allocations.
description: This skill provides a scheduler-backed Jupyter workflow with conservative resources, explicit port and token handling, and tunnel instructions that avoid running notebooks on login nodes.

inputs.name: port
inputs.description: Notebook port, defaulting to a user-selected value such as 8888.

inputs.name: partition
inputs.description: Interactive, debug, CPU, or GPU partition suitable for notebooks.

outputs.name: jupyter_log
outputs.description: Slurm output containing compute host, selected port, and Jupyter URL.

outputs.name: tunnel_command
outputs.description: Template SSH port-forwarding command to run from the user's laptop.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Submit a short notebook job, confirm the log prints a compute hostname and tunnel template, then cancel the job.
